data.title: Energy Support Measures May Intensify Fiscal Challenges in France and UK
data.description: Energy support measures implemented by countries like France and the UK could exacerbate their fiscal challenges, according to Jin10. The head of Fitch Ratings for Western Europe highlighted concerns over the financial implications of these measures. As governments strive to alleviate the impact of rising energy costs on consumers, the increased expenditure may strain national budgets. This situation poses a significant challenge for policymakers as they balance economic support with fiscal sustainability. The ongoing energy crisis has prompted various nations to introduce subsidies and financial aid, aiming to cushion the blow for households and businesses. However, the long-term effects on public finances remain a critical issue, with potential repercussions for economic stability and growth.

data.title: U.S. Energy Secretary Wright: Gasoline Price Impact to Last Weeks, Not Months
data.description: U.S. Energy Secretary Wright has indicated that the impact on gasoline prices is expected to persist for several weeks rather than months. According to Jin10, this statement comes amid ongoing discussions about energy supply and pricing dynamics. The duration of the price impact is a key concern for both consumers and policymakers as they navigate the current economic landscape. Wright's comments aim to provide clarity on the expected timeline for price stabilization in the energy sector.

data.title: Federal Reserve Governor Waller: War is unlikely to lead to sustained inflation
data.description: Federal Reserve Governor Waller stated that war is unlikely to cause sustained inflation, which is why we never look at energy prices. (Jinshi)

data.title: Analysis: Bitcoin surged and then fell back, breaking below $70,000, with institutional spot buying and derivatives short selling locked in a stalemate.
data.description: Bitcoin has fallen below $70,000, and the market is currently showing a clear divergence. Institutional spot buying continues to accumulate Bitcoin, while derivatives traders are constantly increasing their short positions. Historically, when spot accumulation and negative funding rates occur simultaneously, it often triggers a "short squeeze," where short sellers are forced to close their positions, pushing prices upward. However, this outcome is not inevitable. Analysts believe that this pullback mainly reflects profit-taking pressure from short-term traders. Some investors chose to cash out after buying during the rebound. Although there has been a recent rebound, the market still lacks sufficient confidence in whether the upward trend can be sustained. The derivatives market sentiment is also pessimistic, with funding rates remaining significantly negative, meaning that traders are paying fees to maintain their short positions, but at the same time, spot demand remains. Recently, the amount of stablecoins flowing into exchanges has reached a new high since 2026, and spot Bitcoin ETFs have also seen a return to net inflows. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Analysts: A brief conflict may limit the upside for the US dollar index; a blockade of the Taiwan Strait remains the biggest variable.
data.description: Ebury strategist Matthew Ryan stated in a report that the dollar's recent gains may have limited room to continue if the Middle East conflict is relatively short-lived. He pointed out that the market is currently pricing in a conflict that could last for about a month, based on signals from Trump. In this scenario, the dollar's gains should be limited, and a pullback is likely once the conflict ends. However, broader regional involvement and the continued blockade of the Strait of Hormuz "pose a clear risk." He believes this would lead to a further surge in oil prices, thereby boosting the dollar and impacting risk-sensitive currencies. (Jinshi)

data.title: Binance released its February 2026 reserve verification data: BTC reserves reached 100.05%.
data.description: Binance released its reserve verification data for February 2026, showing that the reserve ratio for BTC reached 100.05%, the reserve ratio for ETH reached 100%, the reserve ratio for USDT reached 105.09%, and the reserve ratio for BNB reached 101.14%.

data.title: India's Central Bank to Purchase Bonds Worth 1 Trillion Rupees in Two Phases
data.description: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has announced its plan to acquire bonds totaling 1 trillion rupees through open market operations. According to Jin10, this strategic move is aimed at managing liquidity and stabilizing the financial markets. The bond purchases will be executed in two phases, reflecting the central bank's commitment to ensuring adequate liquidity in the banking system. This decision comes amid ongoing efforts to support economic growth and maintain financial stability in the country. The RBI's actions are closely watched by investors and analysts, as they could have significant implications for interest rates and market dynamics.

data.title: Germany Pledges Additional Humanitarian Aid to Middle East
data.description: Germany's Foreign Minister has announced a commitment to provide nearly 100 million euros in humanitarian aid to the Middle East. According to Jin10, this funding aims to support ongoing relief efforts in the region, addressing critical needs arising from conflicts and crises. The aid package underscores Germany's continued dedication to international humanitarian efforts and its role in promoting stability and recovery in affected areas. This initiative is part of a broader strategy to enhance cooperation and support for vulnerable populations in the Middle East.

data.title: Axel Springer to Acquire Telegraph Media Group for £500 Million
data.description: German media publisher Axel Springer is poised to purchase the Telegraph Media Group for £500 million, according to a report by the Financial Times. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the significant acquisition in the media industry. Axel Springer, known for its expansive portfolio, aims to strengthen its presence in the UK market through this strategic move. The acquisition underscores the ongoing consolidation trend within the media sector, as companies seek to expand their reach and influence. The deal is expected to enhance Axel Springer's capabilities and offerings, aligning with its growth objectives. Further details regarding the transaction and its implications for both companies are anticipated in the coming weeks.

data.title: BE Semiconductor Shares Drop Amid Potential Shift in Memory Chip Standards
data.description: BE Semiconductor's stock experienced a significant decline, falling by up to 17% following reports that major memory chip manufacturers are considering new standards. Bloomberg posted on X that these potential changes could postpone the demand for BE Semiconductor's technology. The Dutch company, known for its advanced semiconductor equipment, faces uncertainty as the industry evaluates these new standards. This development has raised concerns about the future demand for BE Semiconductor's products, impacting investor sentiment and leading to the sharp drop in share prices.

data.title: Commodity Market Faces Uncertainty Amid Geopolitical Tensions
data.description: The Zhengzhou Commodity Exchange has issued a notice highlighting increased uncertainty in the commodity market following recent military actions by the U.S. and Israel against Iran. According to Jin10, this geopolitical tension has led to significant price volatility in futures markets, including caustic soda. The exchange has urged its member units to enhance investor education, advising them to participate in trading prudently and within their risk tolerance. Additionally, the exchange emphasized the importance of compliance and risk prevention in investor trading behavior. The Zhengzhou Commodity Exchange is committed to intensifying market monitoring efforts and will strictly address any violations in trading activities.

data.title: Former HKEX CEO: Tokenization cannot reduce the risks of underlying real-world assets.
data.description: Li Xiaojia, former CEO of the Hong Kong Stock Exchange and co-founder and chairman of DripNet, responded to the question of whether DripNet, after launching its private equity fund process, might follow the new regulatory rules issued by the China Securities Regulatory Commission in February this year to be the first in the market to "produce" the tokenization of real-world assets (RWA). He stated that DripNet currently has no need for RWA tokenization and there is no need to issue RWA tokens, as tokenization cannot reduce the risk of the underlying real-world assets. Li Xiaojia also divided virtual asset investors into five categories: the first category is the earliest entrants, the second category is mid-term entrants, the third category is traditional financial institutions, the fourth category is traditional financial professionals with decentralized concepts, and the fifth category is what people commonly refer to as "leeks" (new investors who are easily exploited). (Caixin.com)

data.title: Analysis: US crude oil futures break $85 for the first time in nearly two years, disrupting the global energy "lifeline".
data.description: US crude oil futures broke through $85 a barrel for the first time in nearly two years, with the Middle East war triggering severe turmoil in the energy market and shipping in the Strait of Hormuz almost completely halted. With no signs of abating, Goldman Sachs warned that oil prices risk breaking $100 if supply disruptions continue; European diesel futures rose by more than 50% in a single week; and central banks around the world expressed unease about a possible resurgence of inflation. Qatar's energy minister even warned that oil prices could reach $150. Faced with importers' difficulties in obtaining crude oil, the US Treasury Department's Office of Foreign Assets Control issued a short-term exemption, allowing India to purchase Russian crude oil. As the escalating conflict restricted Middle East supplies, Saudi Arabia raised its April prices for its main crude oil products sold to Asia by the largest margin since August 2022. Riyadh is also diverting millions of barrels of crude oil to its Red Sea ports to bypass the Strait of Hormuz. (Jinshi)

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Drops Below 70,000 USDT with a 3.97% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Mar 06, 2026, 12:08 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has dropped below 70,000 USDT and is now trading at 69,993.648438 USDT, with a narrowed 3.97% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: EU Considers Financial Support to Restore 'Friendship' Pipeline Supply
data.description: The European Union Commission is evaluating potential financial assistance to aid in the restoration of supply through the 'Friendship' oil pipeline. According to Jin10, this move comes as part of efforts to stabilize energy supplies in the region. The pipeline, which plays a crucial role in transporting oil, has faced disruptions, prompting the EU to explore measures to ensure a steady flow of resources. The Commission's consideration of financial support underscores the importance of the pipeline in maintaining energy security and economic stability within the EU.

data.title: BlackRock deposited 2,750 BTC, worth over $190 million, into a Coinbase Prime address.
data.description: According to Arkham's monitoring, BlackRock has just transferred a total of 2,750 BTC, worth approximately $194.04 million, to a Coinbase Prime address through its Bitcoin exchange-traded fund IBIT. Further transactions may follow.

data.title: Turkey Suspends Flights to Middle Eastern Countries Until March 9
data.description: Turkey's Minister of Transport has announced the suspension of flights to and from Iran, Iraq, Syria, Lebanon, and Jordan until March 9. According to Jin10, this decision affects all flights between Turkey and these Middle Eastern countries. The suspension is part of Turkey's broader strategy to address regional concerns and ensure safety in air travel. The move comes amid ongoing geopolitical tensions and aims to mitigate risks associated with travel in the region. Passengers are advised to check with airlines for updates on flight schedules and alternative arrangements.

data.title: Bit Digital disclosed that its ETH holdings increased to 155,434.4 coins at the end of February, worth over $305 million.
data.description: Nasdaq-listed Ethereum treasury company Bit Digital released its holdings update report, showing that as of February 28, 2026, its Ethereum holdings increased to 155,434.4 ETH, worth approximately $305.4 million, with an average purchase price of $3,045. Furthermore, the company disclosed that as of the end of February, it had staked 1,382,696 ETH, representing approximately 89% of its total holdings, generating approximately 313.9 ETH in staking rewards. (PRNewswire)

data.title: Expert: Euro Falls to Four-Week Low Against Pound Amid Energy Price Concerns
data.description: Pepperstone strategist Michael Brown has highlighted the recent decline of the euro against the pound, reaching a four-week low. According to Jin10, this movement is attributed to market perceptions that the eurozone is more vulnerable to energy price shocks compared to the UK. Brown noted that since the onset of the conflict last weekend, market participants have largely viewed developments in the Middle East from this perspective. Additionally, he mentioned that the market has almost fully priced in the expectation of an interest rate hike by the European Central Bank by the end of the year. This anticipation could further pressure the euro, as it may lead to market discounting of growth challenges.
